messages.content:
Pop was originally launched on 29 May 2003 by Chart Show Channels (CSC) Media Group as Toons&Tunes. Toons&Tunes was then later rebranded as its current name, Pop (formerly stylized as pop and POP!) in the following month.

In the channel’s early years, links were presented by Rorry, a lime green dragon with a Scottish accent, who was animated live. He was accompanied by Purrdy, a dragon/cat hybrid who also appeared in the original Tricky programme on ITV1, it was sometimes followed by a programme known as “The Cheeky Monkey Show” which features the live links of the animated coloured monkeys known as “The Cheeky Monkeys,” that was later known by appearing on its sister channel Tiny Pop until its retirement at the end of June 2009.

Originally, the network focused on music videos with animated programming also part of the schedule, but with the numerous music video network options on British television at the time, this focus was quickly abandoned in 2004, when the animated content became much more prominent. Music videos continued to be carried on and off until 2010, before eventually being abandoned entirely.[citation needed, In 2010, Pop had several popular shows, such as The Smurfs, Kikoriki, Oggy and the Cockroaches, etc.

In 2012, Pop introduces semi-mascots known as the MoonGoons, These are animal-like aliens, with the 2 main ones being a blue squid named Boofus and an orange rabbit named Diggle, These were gone by the time of the channel’s next rebrand in 2015, Also in 2014 CSC Media Group had folded into Sony.

On 1 September 2020, Pop rebranded its on-air identity, with the premiere of Bakugan: Armored Alliance. And on the same day, starting with Pokémon Journeys: The Series, the network became the new British home of the Pokémon anime, moving from its previous broadcasters, ITV1 and CITV, of over 20 years. The channel was also sold by Sony to Narrative Entertainment

On 9 November 2022, Narrative Entertainment announced the launch of a new FAST channel, Pop Kids, to stream on Samsung TV Plus, the new FAST network airs shows from Pop and Tiny Pop.

Sometime in 2022, Pop began to branch into YouTube content, as they approached Joe Tasker for a new show called Swipe It! With Joe Taker, they would later acquire from content creators to use on their channel, including LankyBox, Denis and EthanGamer (the latter exclusively airing on Pop Player).

By 2023 Alvin & the Chipmunks, Squish, Miraculous Ladybug and Pokemon were among the channels popular shows,

The channels current mascot is a talking potato from Swipe It!, Also Jamie and Anna from Toonattik as of December 2023

 In September 2023 the channel introduced Toonattik, a revival of the CITV programming block of the same name, Pop
edit
The channel sources its programming from multiple production and distribution studios. Programming includes original shows such as Swipe It! With Joe Tasker, and acquired programming such as Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks, Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir and Pokémon. The channel also includes movies and specials such as the Barbie television films, Pokémon and Monster High.[1]

Zorro: Generation Z. Write a forum convo where the channel is making MAJOR changes, First, The Hollow and Doctor Who are coming on Feb 3, Second, with the exception of Pokemon (which will now have its presence on the channel drastically increased) the current lineup is going Bye Bye, Third, Kikoriki is returning, Fourth, Swipe It is merging with Toonattik, All changes happen on the same day, and Joe Tasker will no longer host the merged show (which will be under the Toonattik name)

**ChannelInsiderFan01**: Woah! Just heard about the MASSIVE changes coming to Pop! The Hollow and Doctor Who starting Feb 3? That’s epic! 😲

**ToonLover82**: Wait, what?! They're ditching almost the entire lineup except for Pokémon, which is getting even more airtime?! That’s a huge shakeup. 🤯

**RetroKid67**: Kikoriki’s back? YES! I’ve missed those little creatures. 😄 But what’s the deal with basically scrapping everything else?

**AnimatedAdventures**: Merge of Swipe It with Toonattik? Without Joe Tasker? That’s a bold move. Curious how they’re gonna blend those vibes. 😕

**ChannelInsiderFan01**: @ToonLover82, yep, seems like they're aiming for a fresh start. Pokémon’s popularity must be off the charts for them to double down like this. But I'm going to miss some of the classics… 😢

**ToonaticFanboy**: @AnimatedAdventures, I have mixed feelings. Swipe It had its charm because of Joe. He brought so much energy! Wonder who’s taking over the merged show 🤔

**RetroKid67**: Is no one else psyched about Doctor Who coming? That’s a big get for Pop! Sci-fi fans are going to be thrilled. TARDIS time, baby! 🚀

**ToonLover82**: I guess they’re trying to revamp the channel entirely – new audiences, perhaps? But the gap Joe Tasker leaves will be HUGE. He had a ton of fans.

**AnimatedAdventures**: True, @ToonLover82. His departure is big news. But I’m mostly worried about losing shows like Miraculous Ladybug and Alvin & the Chipmunks. My daily dose of entertainment is going away. 😟

**ChannelInsiderFan01**: It’s a risky move for sure, but sometimes channels need to evolve. Let’s just hope these big bets pay off for Pop. I’m here for the new era but will def miss some of the old gems. 🌟

**ToonaticFanboy**: End of an era, beginning of a new one. Let’s keep an open mind and see how The Hollow and Doctor Who shake things up. Could bring in a whole new fan base.🤞

**RetroKid67**: Anyone know why such drastic changes though? Feels sudden. But hey, more Pokémon is always good news in my book! Gotta catch 'em all, right? 😉

**AnimatedAdventures**: Change is good… I guess? Will be tuning in Feb 3 to see how all this unfolds. Fingers crossed it’s for the best. 🤞

**ToonLover82**: It’s going to be a VERY interesting start to the year for Pop. Let’s stay tuned and see what happens. Here’s to new beginnings! 🥂
Write a forum convo where a parent tells their kid that Squish is going away "very soon", and wonders what their kid will love now

**DadOfTheYear**: Hey buddy, so I read something today that I think we need to talk about. 😞 It looks like your favorite show, Squish, is going to be off the air very soon. 📺❌

**LittleSquishFan**: What?! No more Squish? But why, dad? I love that show so much! 😭

**DadOfTheYear**: I know you do, champ. Sometimes shows have to end to make room for new ones. It’s just how TV works. But the good news is, there’s so many other amazing shows out there. We can find a new favorite together! 🌟

**LittleSquishFan**: But I love Squish. It’s funny, and the characters are the best. Do you think there’s anything else like it? 😟

**DadOfTheYear**: Absolutely! There are tons of shows with great humor and fun characters. Have you ever seen “Total DramaRama” or “Pokémon”? They could be really fun too. What do you think about giving those a try? 😊

**LittleSquishFan**: I guess “Total DramaRama” sounds funny. And I like Pikachu from Pokémon. Maybe it won’t be so bad... But it won’t be the same without Squish. 😔

**DadOfTheYear**: It’s okay to feel a bit sad, but I promise we’ll find something you love just as much. Plus, we can always rewatch Squish episodes—you know, for the good times. How does a weekend Squish marathon sound before it goes away? 🍿📺

**LittleSquishFan**: That would be awesome, dad! Can we have popcorn and maybe even make some Squish-themed snacks? 🥳

**DadOfTheYear**: You got it! Squish-themed weekend it is! And who knows? The new shows we discover might have some fun snacks we can make too. Let’s dive in and start exploring new adventures together. 🚀💫

**LittleSquishFan**: Okay! Thanks, dad. You always know how to make things better. Maybe the new show could be our new thing. ❤️

**DadOfTheYear**: That’s the spirit! Here’s to new adventures and discovering our next favorite show. Love you, champ! ❤️

**LittleSquishFan**: Love you too, dad! Can’t wait for our TV adventure weekend. 🥰📺

Write a forum convo where other users note that TDR is also leaving, in fact, ALL of the current lineup is toast except Pokemon on Feb 3, and they give some suggestions that the kid will love after Squish goes

**TechMom101**: Just heard about the big shakeup on Pop, Squish AND Total DramaRama (TDR) are going, actually everything but Pokémon. It's going to be tough explaining to the kiddos. 😢 My son is a huge TDR fan. Any suggestions on what might fill that void?

**ComicDad22**: My kids are in the same boat, @TechMom101. We've started exploring other shows with a mix of humor and adventure. Have you considered “Adventure Time”? It's got a great mix of fun and quirky storytelling. 

**GamerParent**: For fans of Squish and TDR, I’d recommend “The Amazing World of Gumball”. It’s hilarious and full of imagination. Kept my two entertained for hours and it's full of episodes!

**SuperMomOf4**: My little ones have taken a liking to “Teen Titans Go!” after we had to say goodbye to some of their old faves. It’s vibrant, energetic, and the episodes are short and sweet. Might be worth a shot!

**BookWormDaddy**: If you're okay with exploring online platforms, “Minecraft: Story Mode” on Netflix is interactive and fun. It allows the kids to make choices that affect the story. It's a big hit in our house for combining gaming and TV time.

**SpaceyCasey**: Can’t go wrong with some classics like “SpongeBob SquarePants”. It’s timeless humor that even parents can get a chuckle from. Also, “Pokémon” isn’t going anywhere, maybe diving deeper into that universe could spark some new interests?

**TechMom101**: These are wonderful suggestions, everyone! Really appreciate it. “Adventure Time” and “The Amazing World of Gumball” sound like they have potential for us. And exploring more of Pokémon could be a good move. Thanks all!

**RecipeQueen**: Don’t forget about checking out some educational yet fun shows too! “Octonauts” and “Wild Kratts” can be surprisingly entertaining and informative. Might be a good way to mix things up from the usual.

**NerdDad**: If they liked Squish for the animated aspect, “Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir” might be a hit. It's got a good blend of superhero action and relatable characters.

**TechMom101**: Thanks, everyone, for pitching in. It’s going to be an adventure discovering new shows with the kids, but I’m feeling more optimistic with all your suggestions. Here’s to new favorites and making more memories! 🌟

Write a tweet saying Pop is giving a farewell marathon to the departing shows from midnight to 7am, starting with Alvin and ending with Squish, Also list the new Pop, 1 show returning, 1 show becoming more prevalent, and 2 new shows, one is a institution

🎉 Say goodbye in style with our farewell marathon 🌙. From midnight to 7am, we're sending off our beloved shows starting with Alvin 🐿️ and ending with Squish 🦠. But as we bid adieu, get ready for the new Pop 🌈: Kikoriki returns 🌀, Pokémon takes center stage ⚡, and introducing two fresh adventures: Classic Doctor Who is back for all time travelers 🕰️ & don't miss the mind-bending mysteries in The Hollow 🔍. A new era begins! #PopTV #FarewellMarathon #NewBeginnings

Write a blog post where ads are already running on the channel, (incidentally Toonattik is seen in the promo) and this confirms that DW is running the Eccleston-now episodes, so no Tom Baker  😢, Also a soon to be ex Alvin fan is getting ready for Kikoriki

### A New Dawn on Pop: Embracing Change and Welcoming New Adventures

As the television landscape constantly evolves, so too do our viewing habits and the channels that entertain us. Pop, a cornerstone of children's programming, is on the cusp of one of its most significant transformations yet. What makes this shift unique is not only the shows departing but the fresh faces and timeless heroes set to fill the void.

**A Fond Farewell Marathon**

Before we dive into the new, let's talk about the send-off being prepared for the beloved shows we're parting with. Pop has planned a touching farewell marathon, running from the magic hours of midnight to 7 am, where fans can binge-watch their favorites one last time. It kicks off with the mischievous antics of "Alvin and the Chipmunks" and culminates with the lovable squishiness of "Squish". It's an opportunity for viewers to say their goodbyes and celebrate the joy these shows brought into their lives.

**The Dawn of a New Era**

The promo reels running on the channel have not only given us a glimpse into the marathon but have also confirmed the arrival of new and returning shows to the Pop lineup. An observant eye might have caught a fleeting glimpse of "Toonattik" in the promos, hinting at not just new content, but a revival of classic formats that have resonated with audiences in the past.

More notably, for the Whovians among us, the ads have dashed the hopes of seeing the iconic Tom Baker episodes on Pop. Instead, the channel confirms that "Doctor Who" will feature the modern era, starting with Christopher Eccleston. While we may mourn the absence of Baker's scarf-wrapped adventures, Eccleston's tenure as the Doctor brought a fresh and thrilling energy to the series that rejuvenated its legacy. It's a journey well worth embarking on, from the emergence of the Bad Wolf to the heart-wrenching departure of Rose Tyler.

**New Shows on the Horizon**

Among the fresh faces, "Kikoriki" is making a notable return. Known for its quirky characters and insightful stories, it's a welcome comeback that promises laughter and lessons in equal measure. For those who've cherished the adventures of Alvin and his brothers, Kikoriki offers a world filled with humor, heart, and a unique charm that captivates both young and old audiences alike.

But let's not overlook the newcomers - "The Hollow" emerges as a new offering filled with mystery and adventure, promising intriguing narratives and compelling characters. It's a series that encourages viewers to unravel puzzles alongside its protagonists, keeping you guessing at every turn.

**Looking Ahead**

Change, while bittersweet, brings with it the opportunity for new favorites to emerge and new stories to unfold. As we bid farewell to the shows we've grown to love on Pop, we also open our hearts and screens to the next generation of programming. From the comedic exploits of "Kikoriki" to the enigmatic journey through "The Hollow", plus the revival of "Toonattik" and the new voyages with "Doctor Who", Pop is set to offer a rich and diverse palette of entertainment.

So, to the soon-to-be ex Alvin fans, I say: gear up for Kikoriki and the adventures that lie ahead. And to all the Whovians, the time is now to rediscover the magic of the Doctor's journey, starting anew with Eccleston. Here's to embracing change, welcoming new adventures, and keeping the spirit of beloved characters alive in our memories. The dawn of a new era on Pop promises to be nothing short of spectacular. 🌟
Write a tweet saying DW airs at 7pm, the new lineup starts at 7am, say the first show to air, and also saying the Hollow airs at 11am, meaning most of the lineup is dominates by 2 shows on loop

🌅 New dawn on Pop starts 7am with the return of #Kikoriki 🌀, a day filled with adventures! 🚀 Catch #TheHollow at 11am - mysteries await! 🕵️‍♂️ Majority of our lineup is now a thrilling loop of these 2 shows 🔄. Don't miss #DoctorWho at 7pm, new journeys every night! 🌌 #PopTVNewEra
Write the early part of the Feb 3 lineup, starting with the final ever Squish at 6am, also Toonattik at 7:25, 9:25 has Pokemon Advanced

🎉📺 Pop TV Schedule for Feb 3: A Day of Goodbyes & New Beginnings! 📺🎉

6:00 AM - The FINAL ever episode of Squish 🦠: Don't miss your last chance to dive into the squishy, fun world of our beloved characters. Say a heartfelt goodbye to a show that's brought smiles to faces everywhere. #FarewellSquish

7:00 AM - **Introduction to the New Dawn**: A special 25-minute segment walking you through the exciting new changes and what to expect from our refreshed lineup. A not-to-be-missed for all our loyal viewers! 🌅

7:25 AM - **Toonattik**: Dive back into this iconic favorite as Toonattik makes its grand return to our screens! Filled with laughter, games, and the coolest cartoons, it's the perfect way to kickstart the brand new Pop era. 🎉📺

9:25 AM - **Pokémon Advanced**: The journey continues with Ash, Pikachu and friends in the Pokémon Advanced series. Get ready for more battles, badges, and Pokémon in this thrilling next chapter! ⚡️🔴

Join us on this exciting day as we say farewell to the classics and usher in a new wave of entertainment on Pop. Don't forget, the action starts bright and early at 6:00 AM with the final episode of Squish. We're bringing the energy, the nostalgia, and loads of new adventures—make sure you're tuned in! 🌟 #PopTV #NewBeginnings #Toonattik #PokemonAdvanced
Write a tweet to Squish fans advising them to tune into another show if they still want funny fun

🌟 Missing Squish? Fear not! For all the funny fun and quirky adventures you loved, tune into #Kikoriki 🌀 starting Feb 3 on Pop! It's packed with laughter, friendship, and wild escapades that'll keep the spirit of fun alive. Don't miss out! 📺😄 #FunnyFunContinues #NewBeginnings
Write a tweet saying "Our lineup may have taken a massive beating this week"

🚨 Our lineup may have taken a massive beating this week 😭, but keep the faith, Pop fans! We're down but not out - bringing you fresh adventures and familiar faces to love 💪🌟. Stay tuned for epic comebacks and new discoveries! 📺✨ #PopTVResilience #StayTuned
Write a forum convo saying "Pop's lineup is almost beaten to death this Saturday, but suprises save it"

**CartoonCraze123**: Hey everyone! Noticed that Pop's lineup seemed almost beaten to death this Saturday? Was starting to think there'd be nothing left to watch. 😟

**SurpriseFanatic**: Totally felt the same, @CartoonCraze123! It was looking grim for a minute there. But then, those surprises absolutely saved the day! 🎉

**AnimationAdmirer**: Right? I was about to switch off, but then Kikoriki came on, and I was hooked. Didn’t see that coming! Plus, Doctor Who in the evening was the cherry on top. 🍒

**SaturdaySavior**: The surprise factor really did it for me. Just when I thought Pop had completely lost the plot, they pulled those rabbits out of the hat. Brilliant strategic move on their part. 👏

**HopefulViewer**: Does anyone know if this means more surprises are in store for us in the future? I’m here for the roller coaster ride if Pop keeps this up. 🎢

**RetroToonLover**: Those surprises gave me hope. Maybe Pop’s playing the long game here, shaking things up to introduce even more variety. Can’t wait to see what they have lined up next. 🌟

**CreativeSpeculation**: Imagine if this is Pop's way of teasing us with pop-up specials or unexpected returns of beloved shows. That would be an epic way to keep viewers on their toes. 🕵️

**SaturdaySavior**: @CreativeSpeculation Love that idea! Unpredictable programming could become Pop’s new thing. Keeps everyone guessing and tuning in for the surprises. Genius! 💡

**AnimationAdmirer**: One thing’s for sure, this Saturday has changed how I view Pop’s lineup. They might seem down at times, but they're definitely not out. Here’s to more surprises and epic comebacks! 🥂

**CartoonCraze123**: Glad we’re all on the same page here. Let’s keep an eye out and support Pop. Who knows what they’ll surprise us with next weekend! 📺🎉

Write a follow up where the new schedule seems to be permanent and Feb 13's schedule initially had the previous shows, but has since been updated, incidentally the user was looking at Alvin when it was changed

**CartoonCraze123**: Hey everyone! Remember our chat about Pop’s lineup and all those surprises last Saturday? Well, it seems there’s another twist! Looks like the new schedule is becoming permanent. 😲

**SaturdaySavior**: Permanent?! That's huge. I was getting used to the idea of surprises, but this is a whole new level. Did they announce it officially?

**AnimationAdmirer**: Didn't see any formal announcement, but here’s the scoop: I was checking the guide for Feb 13, hoping some of the previous shows like Alvin might make a comeback. And guess what? The schedule initially listed the old lineup, but in real-time, it flipped to the new shows!

**SurpriseFanatic**: Wait, you saw it change while you were looking at it? That’s kind of spooky but also super cool! 😄 So, Alvin’s slot was replaced? With what?

**CartoonCraze123**: Exactly! I was on Alvin’s time slot, and boom, it changed to Kikoriki. Seems like they're not just testing waters anymore; they're diving in with this lineup.

**HopefulViewer**: This is bitter-sweet. I'll miss shows like Squish and Total DramaRama, but it's exciting to see Pop taking bold steps. Does anyone know if this lineup includes new episodes or reruns of the new shows?
